title: LoopSpec
description: Specification for a loop-shaped agentic work item.

kind.description: The loop kind. Determines required fields and terminal semantics.

name.description: Slug identifier for this loop instance. Filesystem-safe.

metric.description: JSON field name to extract from evaluate command output.

baseline.description: Current measured value. Must be set before dispatch.

evaluate.description: Shell command that outputs JSON containing the metric field. Must be read-only.

target_files.description: Files the loop worker may modify (L2+). Empty at L1.

human_gated.description: ClarificationKind loops always require human confirmation to terminate.

ExecutorSpec.description: Declares who — or what — takes the action on each turn. An executor receives the turn's context, acts, and returns a TurnResult. The contract is executor-agnostic: shell (deterministic), hermes (reasoning), http (over the wire), and human (judgment). The human type is the general case the others specialize; paired with `level` it encodes the handoff arc human → hermes/L1 → L2 → L3.

type.description: Who acts on each turn. human requires `who`; hermes requires `profile`; shell requires `command`; http requires `url`.

who.description: human: identifier of the person taking the turn.

command.description: shell: executable + args, invoked with LOOP_* env vars.

url.description: http: endpoint that receives TurnContext JSON and returns TurnResult JSON.

TurnResult.description: The outcome of a single executor turn. Every executor — shell, hermes, http, or human — returns one. The outcome vocabulary is richer than pass/fail because non-compliance is the signal the loop learns from: an executor that could only report success would hide where the proposed action was wrong.

outcome.description: applied=did it as-is; modified=did it differently (better local info); rejected=declined (highest-signal — proposal wrong or out-of-band knowledge); failed=attempted, did not complete.

notes.description: Free-text account. For modified and rejected this is the training signal — why the executor diverged from the proposal.

metric_value.description: Measured metric after the turn, if the loop is metric-shaped. null for loops with no scalar metric.

TerminalConditions.description: Conditions under which the loop terminates.

max_iterations.description: Hard cap on iterations. Required — no open-ended loops.

plateau_count.description: Stop after N consecutive iterations with no measurable improvement.

target_score.description: Optional: stop when metric exceeds (or falls below) this value.
